about_us: At Stackular, we are more than just a team – we are a product development community driven by a shared vision. Our values shape who we are, what we do, and how we interact with our peers and our customers. We're not just seeking any regular engineer; we want individuals who identify with our core values and are passionate about software development.

questions.label: Role Description
questions.help: Describe the role in your own words. Include responsibilities, what the person will work on, technologies they'll use, and any special requirements. LLM will analyze this to generate skills and detailed responsibilities.
questions.example: We're looking for an AI/ML engineer to work on autonomous agents. You'll design and experiment with AI agents, build workflows using LangChain and RAG pipelines, integrate APIs and databases, run experiments, and contribute to innovation projects. Should have strong Python skills, ML fundamentals, and familiarity with LLMs.

note: HR fills 8 basic fields + role description. LLM analyzes the role_info to generate: responsibilities, required skills, nice-to-have skills, and target audience.

about_us: At Stackular, we are more than just a team – we are a product development community driven by a shared vision. Our values shape who we are, what we do, and how we interact with our peers and our customers. We're not just seeking any regular engineer; we want individuals who identify with our core values and are passionate about software development.

questions.label: Role Overview (What is this role about?)
questions.example: We're looking for driven interns who want to work on cutting-edge Agentic AI systems—autonomous agents that can reason, plan, and act independently.

questions.label: Key Responsibilities (comma-separated or newline-separated)
questions.example:
Designing and experimenting with autonomous AI agents
Building agent workflows using LangChain, OpenAI tools, RAG pipelines
Integrating APIs, vector databases, and automation tools

questions.label: Key Technologies/Tools (comma-separated)
questions.example: LangChain, OpenAI, RAG, Python, Vector Databases

questions.label: Required Skills (comma-separated or newline-separated)
questions.example:
Strong Python skills
Understanding of ML fundamentals & embeddings
Familiarity with LLM-based apps

questions.label: Nice-to-Have Skills (comma-separated or newline-separated)
questions.example:
Experience with autonomous agents (CrewAI, AutoGen)
Exposure to vector databases
Knowledge of reinforcement learning

questions.label: Who Can Apply (target audience/eligibility)
questions.example:
Final-year students or recent graduates in Computer Science, AI, Data Science
Candidates with hands-on coursework or project experience in AI/ML
